title: 9 Best Herbal Teas For Urinary Calculus
intro: Herbal teas for Urinary calculus are natural remedies used to treat kidney stones, also known as urinary calculus, which are hard mineral deposits that form in the kidneys and can cause severe pain and discomfort. These herbal teas can treat this ailment by helping to break down and eliminate the stones, reducing inflammation and pain, and promoting overall kidney health. Some examples of herbal teas used to treat urinary calculus include juniper berries, which are known for their diuretic properties that help to flush out the stones; uva ursi, which contains tannins that help to reduce inflammation and fight off infections; corn silk, which is rich in mucilage that helps to soothe and protect the kidneys; and cleavers, which is a natural diuretic that helps to increase urine production and flush out the stones. Additionally, other herbal teas such as parsley, dandelion root, and gravel root are also used to treat urinary calculus due to their ability to help break down and eliminate the stones, reduce inflammation, and promote overall kidney health.

preparations.herb_desc: Juniperus communis teas, derived from the juniper tree, have been used to treat the urinary calculus ailment due to their diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by increasing urine production, reducing inflammation, and preventing the formation of kidney stones, thereby alleviating symptoms such as pain, nausea, and difficulty urinating. The bioactive constituents of Juniperus communis teas, including alpha-pinene, beta-pinene, and terpinolene, contribute to its therapeutic effects by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and reducing oxidative stress in the urinary tract. The benefits of using Juniperus communis teas to treat urinary calculus include improved urine flow, reduced risk of kidney damage, and relief from painful symptoms associated with kidney stones.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Juniperus communis berries and 1 cup of water.
Boil the water in a pot and add the dried berries.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the berries. Let it cool.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help prevent urinary calculus.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Juniperus communis teas can be used to treat urinary calculus, but consuming them may lead to side effects such as nausea, dizziness, and changes in blood pressure due to their diuretic properties. To use Juniperus communis teas safely, take precautions by drinking them in moderation, avoiding excessive consumption, and monitoring urine output to prevent dehydration.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor or herbalist before using Juniperus communis teas
Take 1 cup of Juniperus communis tea 2-3 times a day
Use 2-3 tablespoons of dried Juniperus communis leaves per cup
Continue usage for 2-4 weeks or as advised by your doctor
Monitor urine output and consult doctor if no improvement after 2 weeks

preparations.herb_desc: Berberis vulgaris teas, also known as barberry tea, have been traditionally used to treat urinary calculus, a condition characterized by the formation of kidney stones. The diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to treat this ailment by increasing urine production and reducing inflammation in the urinary tract. The bioactive constituents, including berberine and isoquinoline alkaloids, exhibit antibacterial and antifungal activities that help to prevent the formation of kidney stones. The benefits of using Berberis vulgaris teas to treat urinary calculus include its ability to reduce the risk of stone recurrence, alleviate symptoms such as pain and discomfort, and promote overall urinary tract health.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of fresh Berberis vulgaris roots, clean and chop them into small pieces.
Measure 1 tablespoon of chopped roots and put them in a pot.
Add 1 cup of water to the pot and bring it to boil.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1/2 cup, 2-3 times a day, for urinary calculus relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the provided study, Berberis vulgaris teas for urinary calculus are widely used in the Homeopathic system of medicine for treating kidney pain and removal of kidney stones.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Berberis vulgaris teas can be used to treat the urinary calculus ailment, but they may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications due to the presence of berberine. Precautions should be taken to avoid using Berberis vulgaris teas in large quantities or for extended periods, as this can lead to toxicity and exacerbate kidney problems.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Berberis vulgaris tea for urinary calculus.
Take 1-2 teaspoons of Berberis vulgaris tea in 1 cup of hot water 2 times a day.
Drink the tea within 30 minutes of preparation for maximum benefits.
Continue using the tea for 3-4 weeks to see noticeable improvements.
Monitor urine output and kidney function regularly during treatment.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, made from the roots of the licorice plant, have been traditionally used to treat the urinary calculus ailment, also known as kidney stones. The anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties of these teas help to reduce the size and number of kidney stones, making it easier for the body to pass them out. The bioactive constituents of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, inhibit the formation of calcium oxalate crystals, which are a common component of kidney stones. The regular consumption of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas has been found to provide relief from the symptoms of urinary calculus, including pain and discomfort, and may also help to prevent the recurrence of kidney stones.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots.
Cut the roots into small pieces and boil them in 4 cups of water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot, discarding the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the liquid and stir well. Bring the mixture to a simmer for 5 minutes.
Remove from heat and let the tea cool. Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day for urinary calculus relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Pharmacognosy magazine", Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for urinary calculus, as part of the polyherbal decoction SK, have been found to prevent the elevation of renal and urinary stone biomarkers, and to restore antioxidant levels, thereby preventing renal damage and stone formation in rats.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat urinary calculus, but its long-term use may lead to side effects such as increased blood pressure, potassium deficiency, and low levels of aldosterone. To minimize potential side effects, it is essential to consume these teas in moderation, avoid excessive consumption for extended periods, and monitor blood pressure and potassium levels regularly.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 2 times a day.
Use 2 tablespoons of dried root in each cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain before drinking.
Continue for 2-3 months to help dissolve urinary calculus.
Monitor urine output and consult a doctor if issues persist.

preparations.herb_desc: Taraxacum officinale teas, also known as dandelion root tea, have been traditionally used to treat urinary calculus, a condition characterized by the formation of kidney stones. The diuretic properties of this herbal preparation help to increase urine production, which aids in flushing out the stones from the urinary tract. The bioactive constituents, including taraxasterol and sesquiterpene lactones, have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to reduce the size of the stones and prevent further growth. The benefits of dandelion root tea in treating urinary calculus include its ability to alleviate symptoms such as pain and discomfort, as well as its potential to prevent future stone formation by promoting a healthy pH balance in the urine.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers.
Measure 2 tablespoons of dried Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it starts boiling.
Add the 2 tablespoons of dried Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers to the boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink as tea.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Taraxacum officinale teas can help alleviate symptoms of urinary calculus, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions such as skin rashes or itching. When using Taraxacum officinale teas to treat urinary calculus, it's recommended to start with small doses and gradually increase as needed, be cautious of interactions with other medications, and avoid consumption if you have sensitive stomachs or allergies.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Taraxacum officinale tea 2-3 times a day
Use 1 tablespoon of dried Taraxacum officinale leaves per cup
Boil the leaves in 1 cup of water for 5-7 minutes
Drink for 2-4 weeks to see improvement in urinary calculus
Consult doctor for exact dosage and duration of treatment

preparations.herb_desc: Arctium lappa teas have been traditionally used to treat urinary calculus, a condition characterized by the formation of kidney stones. The diuretic and antispasmodic properties of this herbal preparation help to treat this ailment by increasing urine production and alleviating spasms in the urinary tract. The bioactive constituents, including inulin, arctiin, and arctigenin, present in Arctium lappa teas, have been found to inhibit the formation of calcium oxalate stones, thereby helping to treat urinary calculus. The benefits of using Arctium lappa teas to treat urinary calculus include reduced risk of stone formation, alleviation of pain and discomfort, and a natural and non-invasive approach to managing this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Arctium lappa root and 1 cup of water.
Boil the water in a pot and add the dried Arctium lappa root.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the root. Let it cool.
Drink 1 cup of the cooled Arctium lappa tea, 2-3 times a day for relief from urinary calculus.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Arctium lappa teas can be effective in treating urinary calculus due to its diuretic properties, but they may also cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using Arctium lappa teas, including monitoring for kidney damage, as excessive consumption may worsen the condition, and avoiding use in pregnancy and breastfeeding due to potential hormonal interactions.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Arctium lappa teas for urinary calculus.
Drink 1 cup of Arctium lappa tea 2-3 times a day, morning, noon, evening.
Use 1 tablespoon of dried Arctium lappa root per cup of boiling water.
Take the tea for 7-10 days, then rest for 2-3 days, repeat if necessary.
Monitor urine output and consult a doctor if symptoms worsen or do not improve.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas have been used in traditional medicine to treat urinary calculus, also known as kidney stones. The diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties of Curcuma longa teas help to treat this ailment by increasing urine production and reducing inflammation in the urinary tract. The bioactive constituents of Curcuma longa teas, including curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, contribute to its therapeutic effects by inhibiting the formation of kidney stones and reducing oxidative stress. Regular consumption of Curcuma longa teas has been found to provide relief from urinary calculus by dissolving small kidney stones and preventing new stones from forming, making it a beneficial herbal remedy for this condition.

Boil the water in a pot and reduce heat to a simmer.

preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Curcuma longa teas for urinary calculus using its diuretic properties to either dissolve the stones or inhibit the process of urinary stone formation.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be used to treat urinary calculus, but it may cause side effects such as nausea, dizziness, and stomach upset due to its high turmeric content, which can irritate the digestive system. To minimize these side effects, individuals should start with small doses, drink plenty of water, and avoid taking the tea on an empty stomach, as this may help prevent digestive discomfort.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Curcuma longa tea for urinary calculus.
Start with 1 teaspoon of Curcuma longa powder in 1 cup of hot water 2 times a day.
Gradually increase the dosage to 2 teaspoons in 1 cup of hot water 3 times a day.

Use Curcuma longa tea for 3-6 months to see noticeable improvements in urinary calculus.

Regular consumption of Crataegus monogyna teas may help to alleviate symptoms of urinary calculus, such as pain and difficulty urinating, and may even help to prevent the formation of new stones.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Crataegus monogyna leaves and flowers.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the dried herbs in a cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into another cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea and mix well.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, as needed for urinary calculus treatment.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Crataegus monogyna teas can be used to treat urinary calculus, but it may cause side effects such as increased heart rate and blood pressure, as well as gastrointestinal issues like nausea and diarrhea. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation, including avoiding consumption in large quantities and pregnant or breastfeeding women, as well as individuals with cardiovascular conditions or allergies to the plant.

preparations.herb_desc: Lithospermum erythrorhizon teas have been traditionally used to treat the urinary calculus ailment, also known as kidney stones, due to their diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by increasing urine production and reducing inflammation in the urinary tract, making it easier to pass kidney stones. The bioactive constituents of Lithospermum erythrorhizon teas, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that aid in the prevention and treatment of kidney stones. Regular consumption of Lithospermum erythrorhizon teas has been found to provide relief from the symptoms of urinary calculus, such as severe pain and discomfort, and may also help to prevent the formation of new kidney stones.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Lithospermum erythrorhizon leaves.
Combine the leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a saucepan.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup and discard the solids.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for urinary calculus relief.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lithospermum erythrorhizon teas can potentially cause side effects such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and diarrhea, when used to treat urinary calculus, as well as allergic reactions in some individuals. To use this herbal preparation safely, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming it for extended periods of time to minimize the risk of liver and kidney damage.

preparations.herb_desc: Rheum palmatum teas, also known as Chinese rhubarb tea, have been traditionally used to treat the urinary calculus ailment due to their diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat the ailment by increasing urine production and reducing inflammation in the urinary tract, thereby preventing the formation of kidney stones. The bioactive constituents of Rheum palmatum teas, including rhein and emodin, help to inhibit the growth of bacteria and reduce oxidative stress in the urinary tract, further contributing to its therapeutic effects. The benefits of using Rheum palmatum teas to treat urinary calculus include reduced symptoms, improved kidney function, and a lower risk of stone recurrence.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Rheum palmatum roots. Wash them clean.
Dry the roots in a warm place or using a food dehydrator. This should take 2-3 days.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried roots into a powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder.
Combine the powder with 1 cup of boiling water in a saucepan. Let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid and drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help prevent urinary calculus.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of alternative and complementary medicine (New York, N.Y.), Rheum palmatum teas have been shown to possess high antioxidant capacity, ranking among the top 12 herbs for in vitro radical-scavenging activity in the treatment of renal disorders.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rheum palmatum teas can be used to treat urinary calculus, but it may cause side effects such as digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications, which can lead to complications like kidney damage or worsening of the condition. To use Rheum palmatum teas safely, it is essential to take precautions, such as monitoring urine output and kidney function, avoiding excessive consumption, and being aware of potential allergic reactions, particularly in individuals with pre-existing kidney problems or allergies.

preparations.herb_desc: Aralia racemosa teas have been traditionally used to treat urinary calculus, a condition characterized by the formation of kidney stones. The herbal preparation's diuretic properties help to increase urine production, which in turn helps to flush out the stones from the urinary tract. The bioactive constituents of Aralia racemosa teas, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce the risk of stone formation and alleviate symptoms associated with urinary calculus. The benefits of using Aralia racemosa teas to treat urinary calculus include reduced pain, improved urine flow, and a lower risk of complications such as infections and chronic kidney disease.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Aralia racemosa root and 1 cup of water.
Heat the water in a pot over medium heat.
Add the dried Aralia racemosa root to the boiling water.
Let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes, then turn off the heat.
Strain the tea and drink 1/2 cup, 2-3 times a day, as needed.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aralia racemosa teas can be used to treat the urinary calculus ailment, but it may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal upset, nausea, and dizziness in some individuals due to its potential interactions with other medications and health conditions. To minimize potential risks, it's essential to use Aralia racemosa teas in moderation, avoid consuming it in large quantities, and be aware of potential allergic reactions, especially in people with sensitive stomachs or those taking medications for blood thinning or diabetes.

intro_study: According to "MMW, Munchener medizinische Wochenschrift", teas for urinary calculus, specifically Solubitrat medicinal tea, reduce the risk of urinary calculus formation by preventing excretion peaks of lithogenic substances, such as calcium, at night.
